title: test-driven-development

goal.text: Implement behavior through a strict test-first red-green-refactor cycle instead of writing code and testing later.

requirements.summary: Use this before writing implementation code for features, bug fixes, refactors, or behavior changes.
acceptanceCriteria: The workflow enforces a failing test before production code.
Both verify-red and verify-green checkpoints are explicit and mandatory.
Only minimal code is added during GREEN, and refactor happens only after green.
If code was written first, the workflow routes to delete-and-restart rather than adapting tests afterward.

tasks.title: Confirm that this work must start with test-first discipline
tasks.detail: Use this before implementation code for features, bug fixes, refactors, or behavior changes. If production code already exists without a prior failing test, stop and route to delete-and-restart instead of adapting tests after the fact.

nextsteps: Write the minimal failing test first.
Do not write implementation code yet.

delete-and-restart.summary: Production code exists before the failing test, so this is not currently TDD.
nextsteps: Start over from the failing test instead of adapting afterward.

tasks.title: RED: write one failing test and verify it fails correctly
tasks.detail: Write a minimal test for one behavior with a clear behavior-oriented name, then run it and confirm it fails for the expected missing-behavior reason rather than for a typo or setup error.

red-verified.summary: The test failed correctly and now justifies minimal implementation.
nextsteps: Write only the smallest code needed to pass.

fix-the-test-first.summary: The test did not fail correctly yet.
nextsteps: Fix the test until it fails for the expected reason.
Do not move to implementation code yet.

tasks.title: GREEN: write minimal code and verify green cleanly
tasks.detail: Implement the smallest possible production code to pass the failing test, then run the test again and confirm it passes cleanly without breaking related tests. Fix code, not the test, if green is not achieved.

green-verified.summary: The minimal implementation is green and ready for cleanup.

fix-code-not-test.summary: The implementation is not green yet.
nextsteps: Do not weaken or rewrite the test to force a pass.

tasks.title: REFACTOR, repeat, and stop on anti-patterns
tasks.detail: Refactor only after green, keep tests green, and then decide whether another failing test is needed for the next behavior. If you catch rationalizations like 'I'll test after' or 'keep the code as reference', stop and restart instead of continuing pseudo-TDD. Use [testing-anti-patterns.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/test-driven-development/testing-anti-patterns.md) when mock-heavy or test-only design smells appear.

references.why: Preserves the red-green-refactor cycle, restart rules, good-test heuristics, and rationalization traps from the source skill.
Keeps the full original examples, philosophical explanations, and verification checklist beside the converted entry.
Maps source information to the converted homes for verification and maintenance.
Adjacent anti-pattern reference explicitly called out by the source skill.

rules: Do not write production code before a failing test exists and has been watched failing for the right reason.
Do not skip verify-red or verify-green.
Do not keep prewritten implementation code as reference when restarting TDD.
Do not add extra features during GREEN; keep implementation minimal.
Do not refactor before green is established.
Do not treat tests written after implementation as equivalent to TDD.
Treat rationalizations like 'just this once' or 'manual testing is enough' as restart signals.
